# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Stack

[Language and version] / [Framework] / [Database] / [Package manager] / [Key libraries]. One paragraph.

## Structure

- `[src/dir1/]` - [what lives here; only when non-obvious]
- `[src/dir2/]` - [what lives here]
- `[src/dir3/]` - [what lives here]

## Commands

- Dev: `[your dev command]`
- Build: `[your build command]`
- Test: `[your test command]`
- Lint: `[your lint command]`
- Type check: `[your typecheck command]`

## Verification

After every change, run in this order:
1. `[type check command]` - fix type errors
2. `[test command]` - fix failing tests
3. `[lint command]` - fix lint errors

## Conventions

- [Specific convention 1, e.g., "Server Components by default; 'use client' only when needed."]
- [Specific convention 2, e.g., "Use Zod for input validation."]
- [Specific convention 3, e.g., "Co-locate tests next to source: Foo.tsx + Foo.test.tsx."]

## Off-limits

- [Anti-pattern 1 + what to do instead, e.g., "Don't edit prisma/migrations/* directly; generate via pnpm prisma migrate dev."]
- [Anti-pattern 2 + what to do instead, e.g., "Don't push to main; PR-only."]
- [Anti-pattern 3 + what to do instead, e.g., "Don't use any; use unknown and narrow."]

## Git

- Conventional commits: [format you use].
- Branch naming: [pattern you use].
- [PR / review / merge rules].

<!--
Maintainer notes (block-level HTML comments are stripped before injection):
- Keep this file under 200 lines (target under 80).
- Every line should pass the "would removing this cause Claude to make mistakes?" test.
- No aspirations ("we aim for X"). State what is actually enforced.
- No standard-language conventions. List only what differs from defaults.
- No file-by-file tours. List directories only when meaning is non-obvious.
- No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data (this file is committed to the repo).
- No personality / persona instructions ("be a senior engineer").
- For multi-step procedures, use a skill instead.
- For deterministic enforcement, use a hook instead.
- For path-scoped rules, add .claude/rules/<topic>.md with paths: frontmatter.
- For long reference content, use @path imports or pitch ("for X, read docs/Y.md").
